page: Vultr vx1-m-2c-16g-120s is a memory-optimized virtual server belonging to the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC-Turin processor running at 2.0 GHz. The configuration includes 2 shared vCPUs, 2 physical cores, 1 thread per core, and 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Local storage is provided via a bundled 120 GB NVMe SSD.

Benchmark results indicate a strong single-core performance profile, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance scoring 3040.08 and single-threaded bzip3 decompression reaching 14.79 GB/sec. However, due to the shared CPU allocation, multi-core performance is limited, with stress-ng multi-core scoring 3040.08 and Passmark CPU scoring 4126.22. Memory bandwidth for large blocks is average at 44.5 GB/sec read and 39.83 GB/sec write, while database operations and Redis SET operations fall into the lower performance tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this instance offers a high memory density relative to its shared CPU allocation, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require sustained, intensive multi-core processing. It is well-suited for single-threaded applications, lightweight caching layers, static web serving, and basic text or file processing tasks. It is less suitable for multi-threaded database clusters or heavy LLM inference.
description: The Vultr vx1-m-2c-16g-120s is a memory-optimized virtual server from the VX1 Cloud Compute family, featuring an x86_64 AMD EPYC-Turin processor with 2 shared vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM. This configuration yields a high 8.0 GB of memory per core, complemented by 120 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Performance benchmarks show strong single-core capabilities, with single-threaded bzip3 decompression in the top 10% and stress-ng single-core in the top 25%. However, its shared CPU allocation results in weaker multi-core and database performance. This server is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy, low-CPU-utilization workloads. It is best suited for static web serving, single-threaded applications, and lightweight caching.
og_description: Vultr vx1-m-2c-16g-120s is a memory-optimized virtual server featuring 2 shared AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs, 16.0 GB of RAM, and 120 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, suitable for memory-intensive, single-threaded workloads.
meta_description: Vultr vx1-m-2c-16g-120s is a memory-optimized server with 2 shared AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 120 GB NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A memory-optimized shared virtual server featuring high memory density, local NVMe storage, and strong single-threaded processing capabilities.
bullet_points: Powered by AMD EPYC-Turin architecture with 2 shared vCPUs
Equipped with 16.0 GB RAM and a 120 GB local NVMe SSD
Strong single-core performance and top-tier single-threaded decompression
Shared CPU allocation leads to lower multi-core and database throughput
Best suited for static web serving, caching, and single-threaded tasks
